content:
Post 1
ASX 200 surged 170.80 points or 1.98% to 8,804.00, crossing back above its 200-day moving average. Materials led the charge as gold priced at AUD 5,957.88/oz underpinned miner confidence, dragging the broader index to within 4.33% of its 52-week high.

Post 2
Materials was the standout sector, up 4.06%, while Energy slid 0.46% to finish as the session's laggard. GMD added 10.83% to AUD 5.32, riding gold's strength, while IDP Education fell 6.58% to AUD 2.13, a sharp reversal that stood out against an otherwise risk-on tape.

Post 3
Gold in AUD terms at 5,957.88/oz is the level to watch. A sustained move toward 6,000.00/oz would provide fresh fuel for ASX miners early next week. With the index now back above its 200-day moving average, any pullback in gold or softness in overnight US futures could quickly test that technical reclaim.
